journal_entry.description: Discounting of 90-day non-interest-bearing promissory note at bank
journal_entry.rationale: Ford discounted a $100,000 note receivable at the bank 50 days after issue. The note has 40 days remaining (90 - 50 = 40). The bank discount is calculated on the face value for the remaining period: $100,000 × 15% × (40/365) = $1,643.84. Ford receives net proceeds of $98,356.16 in cash. The difference between the note's carrying value ($100,000) and the net proceeds ($98,356.16) is recognized as a loss on discounting ($1,643.84). Under IFRS 9, this qualifies as derecognition, so the note receivable is removed from the books.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Note discounting with derecognition: Cash increases (asset), Loss on Discounting increases (expense), Notes Receivable decreases (asset). Discount calculated as $100,000 × 15% × (40/365) = $1,643.84. Net proceeds = $98,356.16. No tax lines needed as this is a financial transaction, not a purchase or sale of goods/services.
